element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players establish initial infrastructure and spice harvesting on Arrakis

element_introduction_order.element: Unit production and faction-specific unit types
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each House (Atreides, Harkonnen, Ordos) has unique unit composition and abilities to master

element_introduction_order.element: Fog of war and map revelation
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Black shroud obscures unseen map portions; grey fog obscures areas outside unit sight range

element_introduction_order.element: Subfaction alliances (Fremen, Ix, Tleilaxu, Guild, Imperial Sardaukar)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player choices attract or anger subfactions, unlocking special units and affecting story progression

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy House unit capture and acquisition
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Capturing enemy buildings grants access to rival faction units for tactical flexibility
Player decisions shape story outcomes (e.g., Harkonnen: choosing between Gunseng and Copec)
Simultaneous battles against multiple enemy Houses competing for Arrakis control
Genetically engineered Emperor Worm with psychic powers; requires complete faction mastery to overcome

typical_tension_triggers: Fog of war concealing enemy movements
Story branching moments with permanent consequences
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tension through three-faction competition with narrative branching points; culminates in high-stakes finale against genetically engineered threat

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Subfaction locations and optional alliance bases may indicate alternate routes or hidden objectives

difficulty_curve.early_game: Base building and unit production fundamentals with light opposition; subfaction trust-building (Atreides) or internal succession conflicts (Harkonnen) introduce narrative complexity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Three-faction competition intensifies; player must balance multiple subfaction relationships while conducting offensive operations; enemy unit capture becomes viable tactical option
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-front wars against rival Houses; story convergence forces final confrontation; all three campaigns culminate in stranded base scenario on enemy homeworld before final Arrakis return
notable_spikes: Harkonnen internal civil war (Copec vs Gunseng conflict)
Final battles against multiple opponent Houses on their home planets
Emperor Worm defense against psychic threats and mind-control drug effects
Forced base relocation and Smugglers Guild rescue mission

notable_level_patterns.description: Early-to-mid campaign missions designed to establish alliance relationships with factions (Fremen, Ix, etc.). Success opens special unit production and affects narrative branching.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Atreides: Fremen diplomat missions; early alliance-building operations

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign-specific missions where player must navigate factional politics before external competition. Harkonnen campaign features internal succession struggle requiring binary choice.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Harkonnen: Baron Rakan succession (Copec vs Gunseng selection)

notable_level_patterns.description: Mid-to-late campaign stages where all three Houses actively compete for spice control and map dominance. Battles progress from skirmishes to full-scale wars.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaigns reach critical point when player battles both opposing Houses simultaneously

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign structure forces victory against each opponent House on their home planet (Caladan, Giedi Prime, or Ordos Prime). Spacing Guild then strands victorious faction before revealing Emperor Worm plot.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Final three battles against Atreides, Harkonnen, and Ordos home worlds

notable_level_patterns.description: Common ending for all three campaigns: player must destroy genetically engineered Emperor Worm on Arrakis after Smugglers Guild extraction. Incorporates psychic threat mechanics and mind-control countermeasures.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All campaigns: Final Emperor Worm destruction on Arrakis
